model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: UConn is a dominant 2-seed with a 29-5 record, ranked 6th in the AP Poll with an SRS of 22.92 (12th nationally) and an elite defense (DRtg 97.9, 24th), while Furman is a 15-seed ranked 191st in KenPom with a negative efficiency margin (-1.99) and a 22-12 record built in the Southern Conference. The talent and efficiency gap is enormous across every dimension—UConn's adjusted offensive and defensive ratings both vastly exceed Furman's, and the Huskies' Big East-tested roster led by Tarris Reed Jr. and Alex Karaban should overwhelm Furman's frontcourt. While Jaylin Stewart's status is uncertain, UConn's depth and Dan Hurley's championship pedigree make this a near-certain outcome consistent with the approximately 94% historical win rate for 2-seeds over 15-seeds.

reasoningStep.title: East 2/15: UConn vs Furman
reasoningStep.summary: UConn's massive advantages in efficiency, talent, schedule strength, and coaching experience make this a comfortable win over a 15-seed Furman team with a negative efficiency margin.
evidence: UConn: 29-5, AP #6, SRS 22.92 (12th), ORtg 116.5 (54th), DRtg 97.9 (24th), SOS 41st
Furman: 22-12, KenPom #191, AdjEM -1.99, AdjO 107.5 (200th), AdjD 109.5, SoCon tournament champion as 6-seed
UConn injury update: Silas Demary Jr. healthy, Jaylin Stewart questionable but depth mitigates impact
UConn led by Tarris Reed Jr. and Alex Karaban with Dan Hurley's championship coaching pedigree

result.text: Furman vs UConn prediction, pick, odds March Madness first round Skip to main content Home PGA Tour LPGA College Amateur Golf Life Equipment Apparel Newsletter Videos Events Odds Betting USA TODAY Sports Contact Us Furman basketball vs UConn prediction, our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Zachary Huber Greenville News Show Caption Hide Caption Watch coach Bob Richey react to Furman basketball's March Madness bid Furman men's basketball coach Bob Richey speaks at the team's Selection Sunday watch party. "We'll see you at Philly!" The Furman Paladins will face the UConn Huskies in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. Furman, a No. 15 seed, secured its spot by winning the Southern Conference tournament. The Paladins will need to counter UConn's strong frontcourt and physical defense to have a chance at an upset. Key matchups include Furman's Cooper Bowser against UConn's frontcourt and guard Alex Wilkins versus defender Silas Demary Jr. The Furman Paladins men's basketball team will face UConn in the opening round of the NCAA Tournament. The Paladins (22-12) were given the No. 15 seed and will attempt to upset No. 2 UConn (29-5) on March 20 at 10 p.m. on TBS . Furman secured its spot in 2026 March Madness after knocking off top-seeded East Tennessee State in the Southern Conference tournament final as a No. 6 seed. SLING TV: Watch Furman vs. UConn on Sling TV Furman overloaded the Buccaneers with its 3-point shooting, knocking down 10 of its 26 attempts. Asa Thomas, Alex Wilkins, Charles Johnston and Tom House all knocked down a pair of threes. Forward Cooper Bowser also took over in the paint, scoring 21 points and grabbing 11 rebounds to secure his first double-double of the season. In 2023, No. 13 Furman upset No. 4 Virginia, thanks to a late 3-pointer from guard JP Pegues. Here is what to know about the Huskies and our prediction for Furman's first-round game. Furman will have its hands full with UConn frontcourt The Huskies are led by center Tarris Reed Jr. and forward Alex Karaban. Reed Jr. leads UConn in scoring and rebounding at 13.7 points and 8.1 rebounds per game. Karaban averages 12 points per game and has made several clutch shots during their national championship runs in 2023 and 2024. Forwards Cooper Bowser, Charles Johnston and Ben Vander Wal will be tasked with slowing down UConn's top front-court players. For the Paladins to have a chance at the upset, it's imperative that Bowser, Johnson and Vander Wal stay out of foul trouble. Furman's Alex Wilkins vs UConn's Silas Demary Jr key matchup to watch Wilkins has had a phenomenal freshman season, averaging 17.7 points and 4.7 assists per game. He poured in 34 points and five assists in the Paladins' Southern Conference semifinal win against UNC Greensboro. Guard Silas Demary Jr. is considered UConn's top defender. He averages 1.7 steals per game and is a top-15 finalist for the Naismith Defensive Player of the Year award. Demary has helped coach Dan Hurley's team be the 11th

"We'll see you at Philly!" The Furman Paladins men's basketball team learned who it will face in the first round of the NCAA Tournament on March 15. Furman is the No. 15 seed in the East Region and will face No. 2 Connecticut on March 20 at 10 p.m. on TBS . The Paladins locked themselves into March Madness for the eighth time in program history and their first appearance since 2023 after winning the Southern Conference tournament championship. SLING TV: Watch Furman vs. UConn on Sling TV As a No. 6 seed in the SoCon tournament, they upset No. 1 East Tennessee State 76-61 in the championship game on March 9. Asa Thomas, Alex Wilkins, Charles Johnston and Tom House all knocked down a pair of threes. Forward Cooper Bowser also dominated in the paint, scoring 21 points and grabbing 11 rebounds to secure his first double-double of the season. The Paladins will look to pull off another upset against the Huskies. In 2023, No. 13 Furman upset No. 4 Virginia, thanks to a clutch 3-pointer from JP Pegues. BRACKET CHALLENGE: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY’s Bracket Challenge today! Bracket Challenge Sweepstakes | USA TODAY Sport SURVIVOR POOL: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY’s Survivor Pool today! Furman overwhelmed the Buccaneers with its 3-point shooting, knocking down 10 of its 26 attempts. Asa Thomas, Alex Wilkins, Charles Johnston and Tom House all knocked down a pair of threes. Forward Cooper Bowser also dominated in the paint, scoring 21 points and grabbing 11 rebounds to secure his first double-double of the season. The Paladins will look to pull off another upset against the Huskies. In 2023, No. 13 Furman upset No. 4 Virginia, thanks to a clutch 3-pointer from JP Pegues. Zachary Huber is a high school sports reporter for the Hendersonville Times-News. Email him at zhuber@gannett.com or follow him on X @zacharyahuber.
